title: Subctl Policy
description: Schema for subctl policy documents (`policy.toml`, the four merge sources, and the per-team snapshot). Expanded from the truncated example in .orchestration/handoff-pack/02-policy-schema.md §5. The TypeScript types in lib/policy/types.ts are the source of truth; this schema mirrors them for IDE validation. Per HANDOFF_DIGEST §3.1 (D9), the policy modes do NOT control the defang — they layer an additive gate on top.

preset.description: Ecosystem preset to inherit from. The shipped set in v2.7.0 is node/python/generic. Rust/Go are reserved for v2.8.0+. Use `none` to opt out of inheritance entirely (required if you need to weaken a preset's deny_always).

default_mode.description: Default mode when `subctl teams <provider>` is invoked without --mode. Subctl's hardcoded fallback is `gated`.

mode.description: Per-mode configuration tables. Each mode is optional in a source file; a project that only customizes Gated need only declare `[mode.gated]`.

__meta.description: Populated by the policy loader after the merge pass. Source files on disk MUST NOT set this — it appears only on resolved policies in memory and in the per-team snapshot's header comments.

trustedMode.description: Trusted-mode configuration. Intentionally empty in v2.7.0 — trusted has no per-mode config (the defang stays at the worker level, not the mode level). Per docs/policy.md §1.1.

gatedMode.description: Gated-mode configuration. The bulk of the policy surface. Resolution at check time: deny_always > deny_if_arg_contains > allow_pattern > allow.commands > default-deny. Per docs/policy-schema.md §3.

allow.description: Exact-match command names (first whitespace-separated token). No arg checking. Use conservatively; most commands belong in allow_pattern.

allow_pattern.description: The core allowlist. Each entry is a command + arg-list filter with optional deny_if_arg_contains refinement.

deny_always.description: Patterns that override every allow rule. Substring matches are case-sensitive; regex is RE2 syntax (Go-style; no lookbehind). Per docs/policy-schema.md §3.4.

allowPattern.description: A single allow_pattern entry. Per docs/policy-schema.md §3.2: command matches first non-flag token; args[] gates first non-flag argument; deny_if_arg_contains is a second-pass token-substring filter.

allowedScripts.description: `<runner> run <script>` allowlist (npm/pnpm/bun/yarn). Also covers `npm test` (treated as `npm run test`). The hook reads `<cwd>/package.json` at check time.

allowedTargets.description: `make <target>` allowlist. The hook reads `<cwd>/Makefile` or `GNUmakefile` and validates the requested target.

allowedRecipes.description: `just <recipe>` allowlist. The hook may run `just --summary` to enumerate available recipes.

allowedModules.description: `python -m <module>` allowlist. Pairs with `deny_always` regex for `python -c` so the only Python execution path through Gated is `python -m`.

sealedMode.description: Sealed-mode configuration. The worker has no Bash; an MCP server exposes the named tool set. Per docs/policy.md §5 + docs/policy-schema.md §4.

mcp_tools.description: The whitelisted MCP tool names. The v1 set is enumerated in docs/policy.md §5 but the schema does NOT enum-restrict so new tools (added by user MCP servers) don't trip the validator.

test_command.description: Exact string the `test_run` MCP tool will execute. No interpolation, no piping, no `&&` chains. `subctl policy validate` rejects pipelines / chains.

escalation.description: What `policy_request` does when the worker asks for a one-off command.

meta.description: Resolved-policy metadata. Populated by the loader, not by source files. Per docs/policy-schema.md §8 (snapshot header).

allowlistSha.description: First 8 hex chars of the snapshot's allowlist sha256.
